thesis.concept: Optical glass exposes shell, logic, and power as one readable repair sequence.
thesis.signature: An original transmissive handset study rotates around a central repair axis while semantic controls isolate each internal layer.
thesis.aestheticRisk: The object intersects the headline instead of occupying a conventional product card.
thesis.restraint: One optical object, one coral repair axis, no dashboard cards, badges, or glow field.

concepts.name: Optical Repair Axis
concepts.signatureInteraction: Choose shell, logic, or power while one centered transparent object reveals the selected layer.
concepts.domainAnchor: Shell, logic, power, fastener access, and optical thickness determine the object hierarchy and layer controls.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: Seeing each repair layer in place makes the layer map more understandable than an exploded marketing render alone.
concepts.tradeoff: A single optical body prioritises spatial comprehension over exhaustive component documentation.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the transmissive handset resembles an existing product or if refraction makes the controls harder to read.

concepts.name: Repair Ledger
concepts.signatureInteraction: Step through a horizontal exploded diagram that connects each part to a repair note.
concepts.domainAnchor: Repair-manual callouts, disassembly order, and fastener locations govern the information architecture.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A familiar manual sequence lowers the interpretation cost for repair-focused visitors.
concepts.tradeoff: The ledger is clearer as documentation but gives up the optical material experience.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if it becomes a generic feature timeline with decorative connector lines.

concepts.name: Refraction Scan
concepts.signatureInteraction: Scrub a narrow optical scan through five material depths without moving the page copy.
concepts.domainAnchor: Material thickness, refractive index, and internal layer depth determine the scan states.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A controlled cross-section makes the difference between surface styling and repair access visible.
concepts.tradeoff: The scan requires more explanation and a carefully authored fallback on unsupported hardware.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the effect reads as an abstract liquid shader rather than a repair instrument.

selectionRationale: Optical Repair Axis preserves the user's see-through material ambition while anchoring every visual layer to a repair concept, keeping copy and controls outside the canvas, and retaining a meaningful SVG cutaway fallback.

typography.display: Arial Narrow / Avenir Next Condensed for the hero; Georgia for editorial sections

system.geometry: one tall optical volume, central repair axis, and circular refractive measures
system.material: a custom double-sided GLSL Fresnel alpha shell, flat internal plates, exposed fasteners, dashed connector, coral halo, and warm power block

architecture.header: A fixed vertical product rail persists across desktop and mobile; the fixed pause or resume control and aligned study footer occupy that reserved gutter outside the object stage.
architecture.heroFrame: A full-viewport object stage lets the transmissive body intersect display typography without containing semantic copy.
architecture.scrollDepth: The spatial object resolves into an explicit layer selector, live layer description, text-led material principle, and the shared study-route footer.
architecture.mobileMutation: The rail narrows but remains vertical, the same layer buttons drive a reduced-tessellation one-shot object, and the study footer becomes a two-column route grid inside the remaining content width.

graphics.justification: A custom double-sided GLSL shell uses view-dependent Fresnel colour and alpha to keep the fictional fasteners, connector, logic plane, power cell, and lens legible through a stylised transparent body.

fallbacks.noWebGL: An authored SVG cutaway replaces the canvas and labels shell, logic plane, and power cell through adjacent semantic copy.
fallbacks.reducedMotion: The object renders a static optical angle; layer buttons update discrete internal emphasis without ambient rotation or float.
fallbacks.lowPower: The Three.js path uses lower curve segments, a capped render budget, and one-shot rendering while preserving the same semantic layer controls.
fallbacks.assetFailure: The complete layer selector, live text description, material principle, and actions remain usable without the optical object.

motion.entrance: The semantic headline, explanation, and action render before the lazy optical object.
motion.nicheMoment: Layer buttons isolate shell, logic, and power inside the same transmissive body while pointer parallax remains optional.
motion.reducedMotionStrategy: Snap to one optical angle, remove ambient float and rotation, and retain instant button-driven layer states.

claims.text: The Vitreum page labels the handset study as a fictional original concept and not an existing product.
claims.scope: The claim verifies the page's disclosure language only; it is not an external novelty or product-search finding.

reports.notes: Three desktop runs per route; the release gate recomputes median LCP, CLS, and TBT against this exact build.
The browser report records complete tab order, every state-control family, pause/resume, CTA activation, visible focus, and 24-by-24-pixel minimum targets.
The browser report records console and page errors for every route/profile pair.
